master_row.title: Cent Field Strength Relation
master_row.canonical_statement: \begin{theorem}[Opposite Helicity Conservation] North and south polar jets maintain opposite helicities: \begin{equation} \mathcal{H}_{\text{north}} = +\int_{\mathcal{V}_N} \mathbf{A} \cdot \mathbf{B} \, d^3x = -\mathcal{H}_{\text{south}} \end{equation} with centripetal trajectories: \begin{equation} \mathbf{F}_{\text{cent}} = -\frac{mv^2}{r}\hat{\mathbf{r}} + q(\mathbf{v} \times \mathbf{B}) + \frac{2G}{c^2r^2}\mathbf{J} \times \mathbf{v} \end{equation} \end{theorem}
master_row.formation_inputs: m, v, r, q, B, G
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Bidirectional Helical Jets’.
master_row.dictionary: Cent Field Strength Relation — F_cent = -(mv^2)/(r)hatr + q(v × B) + (2G)/(c^2r^2)J × v
master_row.encyclopedia: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `00_Engine\Lemma-Maker\Prism\unified_field_theory_paper_centerpetal.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.

formal_status.note: Proof package captures the theorem row, metadata, source truth, TeX source, and current semantic translation status.
